Recommendations.Action: Immediate forced password reset for all users with dark web / paste site exposure
Recommendations.Rationale: 116 user(s) found on dark web forums or paste sites. This indicates credentials are actively circulating among threat actors.

Recommendations.Action: Priority MFA enforcement and CA policy tightening for Radiology
Recommendations.Rationale: Radiology has a 55.0% credential exposure rate — the highest of all departments.

Recommendations.Action: Enable Entra ID Password Protection with banned-password list
Recommendations.Rationale: Credential dump exposure means plaintext or crackable passwords are in the wild. Password Protection blocks reuse of known compromised passwords.

Recommendations.Action: Audit for username/password reuse across personal and work accounts
Recommendations.Rationale: DeHashed records include usernames and hashed passwords. If work usernames match personal accounts, attackers can attempt credential stuffing.

Recommendations.Action: Enrol all exposed users in Identity Protection risky-user remediation workflow
Recommendations.Rationale: 204 users are exposed across external breach databases. Systematic remediation via Entra ID risk-based policies is required.

Recommendations.Action: Integrate OSINT checks into the joiner provisioning workflow
Recommendations.Rationale: Proactive breach screening at onboarding prevents new hires from bringing compromised credentials into the MediZuva environment.

Recommendations.Action: Schedule monthly OSINT re-scans and feed results into the threat dashboard
Recommendations.Rationale: Breach exposure is dynamic — new leaks surface regularly and require continuous monitoring.
